title: Die Sterne ver 20
wikitext:
[]: Die Sterne ver 2.0             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Apr 19, 2008 8:11 pm Version 1 is found here: http://evacommentary.org/omake/die-sterne/die-sterne.html These are the images not in version 1 P100 P100-1 P100-2 P101 P101-1

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Apr 19, 2008 8:20 pm WOOT! Thank you! I need to buy this book!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 19, 2008 8:27 pm Thanks for doing this, Zeak. Even if the images aren't perfect, it's great to at least know what pics are in the darn thing!

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Sat Apr 19, 2008 9:25 pm above picture is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Apr 19, 2008 9:43 pm The expression on Gendo's face is perfect.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sat Apr 19, 2008 10:31 pm Sugoi! Thanks for uploading, I like the stance that Eva-01 is on.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Apr 20, 2008 4:57 am Whatever your opinion on Sadamoto may be you can't deny guy can draw

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Sun Apr 20, 2008 9:39 am @ Ursus: amen. awesome coloring job, too. just great... also, ...what?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 20, 2008 9:46 am zimlord wrote: ...what?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Apr 20, 2008 9:49 am That's the F-type armor, isn't it? It comes in one of the games.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 20, 2008 5:14 pm The F-Type Armor/Equipment comes from the Eva2 game but was also used in some of those Super Robot Wars games. In terms of the pics, Sadamoto didn't draw the ones Zeak uploaded so far, those were other artists. Anyway, it's nice to see another pic of Shinji holding Misato's cross.

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Sun Apr 20, 2008 9:07 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: The F-Type Armor/Equipment comes from the Eva2 game but was also used in some of those Super Robot Wars games.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 22, 2008 12:52 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: In terms of the pics, Sadamoto didn't draw the ones Zeak uploaded so far, those were other artists.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Apr 22, 2008 1:58 am Im sorry but its hard to scan an A3 size page on an A4 scanner without marking to book. Anyway I have had a very very full on few days. Ill talk about It later. Anyway im not going to be able to do much as this weekend is ANZAC day so its one of the biggest drinking weekends In Australia. I appologise in advance if I manage to offend anybody on Saturday morning on vent or the IRC Im going to be very very drunk and would have not have sleeped in 36 hours. Im going to scan some more images now anyway Ill upload them shortly.

[]:             Quote Post by Steve » Tue Apr 22, 2008 5:14 am Zeak wrote: Anyway im not going to be able to do much as this weekend is ANZAC day so its one of the biggest drinking weekends In Australia.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Apr 22, 2008 5:18 am Steve wrote: Zeak wrote: Anyway im not going to be able to do much as this weekend is ANZAC day so its one of the biggest drinking weekends In Australia.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 23, 2008 7:48 am tell me if you want any images and Ill scan some of them as better copies

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 23, 2008 7:53 am

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed Apr 23, 2008 7:58 am Image in bottom left is on that board at the moment Ill upload the rest later If you want any better scans Just PM me. I don't have time to run off all the scans at the moment (finishing retake) so this is the best way rather than scanning the whole book. Most of the images where on that board at some stage

[]:             Quote Post by zimlord » Wed Apr 23, 2008 8:28 am wow. thanks zeak! some of the Asuka/Rei ones are nice. just awesome. also, i've flipped thru dermond a couple times at borders and what not, and i was just wondering, is die sterne unlike dermond in the fact that it has many artists work? clearly it's all eva, but looks liek there are some different styles in the book. any well known artists? anyway, neat stuff!
